questions.question: What is the primary difference between a D-latch and a D-flip-flop in terms of their triggering mechanism?
options: A D-latch is edge-triggered, while a D-flip-flop is level-sensitive.
A D-latch is level-sensitive, while a D-flip-flop is edge-triggered.
A D-latch can store multiple bits, while a D-flip-flop stores only one.
A D-latch has an asynchronous reset, while a D-flip-flop always has a synchronous reset.
A D-latch is used in combinational logic, while a D-flip-flop is used in sequential logic.

questions.question: In the typical Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) design flow, what is the primary output of the "logic synthesis" stage?
options: A technology-independent Register-Transfer Level (RTL) description.
A physical layout (GDSII file) ready for fabrication.
A gate-level netlist mapped to a target technology library.
A verified test bench for functional simulation.
An optimized floorplan with power grid distribution.

questions.question: In computer architecture, what is the primary characteristic of a "write-back" cache policy when a modified cache block needs to be written to main memory?
options: All write operations are immediately propagated to both cache and main memory.
Data is written to main memory only when the corresponding cache block is evicted or explicitly flushed.
Write operations only update the cache, and main memory is never updated.
Writes are broadcast to all other caches in a multi-processor system before updating main memory.
Main memory is updated only during system shutdown or power-off.

questions.question: In advanced low-power digital IC design, what is the primary purpose of implementing "power gating" techniques?
options: To dynamically adjust the operating frequency of the circuit based on workload.
To reduce static (leakage) power consumption by completely cutting off power to idle blocks.
To minimize dynamic power consumption by disabling clock signals to inactive blocks.
To reduce manufacturing costs by using smaller transistors.
To improve circuit performance by increasing the supply voltage.

questions.question: In the context of large-scale System-on-Chip (SoC) designs, what is the primary advantage of employing a Network-on-Chip (NoC) architecture compared to a traditional bus-based interconnect system?
options: Reduced design complexity due to a centralized control unit.
Enhanced scalability and throughput, especially for many IP cores, by using parallel communication paths.
Lower power consumption because all communication is synchronous.
Guaranteed real-time performance for all data transfers regardless of network congestion.
Simpler arbitration logic and fewer routing challenges.
